mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive forward returns for the S&P 500 over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window or forward returns are negative in 65% of rate decline periods.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 8.0 percent, labor market slack severely constrains corporate earnings growth, triggering negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of periods where UNRATE exceeds 8.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts signals robust aggregate demand, driving positive equity returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST fails to produce positive 60-day returns in at least 60% of observed instances.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during periods of stable inflation indicates healthy capacity utilization without pricing pressure, supporting positive market returns over one month.
failure_condition: Positive INDPRO growth coincides with negative 20-day returns in over 50% of months when CPIAUCSL growth remains below 3.0 percent annually.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly increases in real gross domestic product reflect underlying economic strength that translates into higher corporate profits and positive equity returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Positive GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter growth is followed by negative 5-day returns in 60% of cases over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate above 4.5 percent imposes severe financing constraints on highly leveraged firms, compressing valuations and generating negative forward returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 achieves positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of periods when FEDFUNDS remains above 4.5 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Rapid job creation combined with a falling unemployment rate signals a tightening labor market that boosts consumer spending and drives positive equity returns over one month.
failure_condition: Concurrent PAYEMS growth and UNRATE decline fails to yield positive 20-day returns in at least 55% of observed months.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle reflects demand destruction rather than supply shocks, leading to negative equity returns over the following week.
failure_condition: Negative INDPRO growth during tightening periods produces positive 5-day returns in over 60% of cases, invalidating the demand destruction channel.
